description: Conformance vectors for the R2-USB serial transport framing and properties (R2-USB §3 Framing, §4 Transport Properties). USB carries compact R2-WIRE frames length-prefixed on the serial byte stream. All multi-byte integers on the USB framing layer are LITTLE-ENDIAN (§3.2). Hex is lowercase. Vector ids follow the TV<n> scheme cited by consuming impls.
notes: Frame encoding (§3.2): [payload_length: uint16 LE][R2-WIRE compact message: payload_length bytes]. Max payload 255 (compact). payload_length == 0 is a no-op/keepalive that MUST be silently ignored.

SYNC frame (§3.3): payload_length=4; magic = uint16 0x5232, encoded little-endian -> wire bytes 0x32 0x52 (confirmed against r2-hive SYNC_MAGIC 0x5232 to_le_bytes()); version=0x01; flags (bit0 = extended-format supported, bits 1-7 reserved MUST be 0).
Behavioural vectors describe a required behaviour rather than a single byte string.

re_verify.method: v0.9: prose/notes only — TV27 notes corrected to the R2-BEACON 8.1.1 accepted LoRa length set {15,16,17} (15 core/16 tx_power/17 dev); no vector bytes changed. v0.8: (1) UNIFIED the CAPS descriptor `kind` enum to R2-TRANSPORT §2.2 (was a separate Appendix-A enum: LoRa=1/BLE=2; android caught the two-enums-in-one-protocol inconsistency) -> TV21 `kind` 1->2 (LoRa), TV22 invalid-example likewise; CAPS wire change, pre-release, hive usb.rs re-emits. (2) NEW §3.7.1 observation subtype (msg_type=12) + TV27 (real DEV LoRa observation). Other 26 vectors are §3.2/§3.4/framing tests untouched.
re_verify.verdict: TV21/TV22 re-issued for the §2.2 enum unification; TV27 added. All other vectors byte-identical.

prior.method: v0.7 (proactive cross-repo interop sweep) marks §3.7's Error report (msg_type 1) as explicitly deliberate implementation-defined content, not an accidental gap -- a documentation clarification adding a MUST that receivers treat it as opaque, with no byte-layout or wire-format change. Confirmed: none of this file's 26 vectors exercise msg_type 1 / control frames at all (all are §3.2/§4 framing/length-prefix/transport-property tests per the earlier re_verify), so the change cannot have touched any of them.
prior.verdict: All 26 vectors HOLD vs R2-USB v0.7. No vector value changed. (Prior re_verify for the 0.3->0.6 catch-up preserved below for history.)

prior.method: First re-verify since v0.3 (no prior re_verify block existed). 3 intervening changes, none touching §3/§4 framing: v0.4 added a §3.6 'relation to device_id' cross-ref note (explicitly 'vectors unchanged'); v0.5 dropped a retired DEV_PK token from that same §3.6 cross-ref note (terminology cleanup only); v0.6 added §1 NEW normative host-radio-delegation text (no-BlueZ/all-radio-via-MCU model, explicitly 'No wire/format change'). Confirmed independently: grepped this file for DEV_PK/DEV_SK/device_id -- zero hits; all 26 TV vectors are framing/length-prefix/transport-property byte tests (category: framing etc.), nothing here represents device identity or radio topology, so none of the 3 changes could have touched it either way.
prior.verdict: All 26 vectors HOLD vs R2-USB v0.6 (label-lag catch-up 0.3->0.6, byte-identical). No vector value changed.

vectors.description: Encode a compact R2-WIRE frame onto the USB serial stream (§3.2).
payload_length = 255 is the maximum valid compact payload (§3.2, §4.1). Length prefix shown; payload bytes omitted.
payload_length > 255 exceeds the compact maximum (§3.2, §4.1) and MUST be rejected.
A zero-length frame (payload_length = 0) is a no-op/keepalive and MUST be silently ignored (§3.2, §3.4).
SYNC frame, compact-only (flags = 0x00) (§3.3).
SYNC frame advertising extended-format support (flags bit0 = 1) (§3.3).
SYNC with a reserved flag bit set (flags = 0x02, bit1) is invalid — bits 1-7 MUST be 0 (§3.3).
vectors.reason: reserved flag bits (1-7) must be zero

vectors.description: Receiving a SYNC on an ESTABLISHED link is idempotently RE-ANSWERED, not a reset: the endpoint responds with a SYNC and PRESERVES existing link state (session, negotiated CAPS/activation, negotiated tier, and the link generation (the epoch) + reset_seq). A bare re-SYNC is a liveness signal, NOT a teardown — it MUST NOT by itself reset the link or bump generation/reset_seq (resetting a healthy link on every keepalive is a defect). The link state is reset ONLY by a changed link identity — a CAPS with a different usb_link_id -> LINK RESET (§3.6), which alone bumps generation/reset_seq. Both ends MUST accept a SYNC at any time.
vectors.notes: CORRECTED at v0.25. This vector previously read 'MUST reset the link state and respond' / expect=reset_and_respond — the exact MUST that R2-USB v0.24 reversed as demonstrably wrong (it tore down healthy links on every keepalive: the flap Roy observed live). The v0.24 fold landed the spec correction but left this vector asserting the reversed rule, and its 'label co-bump only, bytes identical' claim was FALSE — TV8 encodes the behaviour v0.24 changed. An implementer conforming to the old TV8 would reproduce the flap. No known consumer pinned TV8 (r2-android core-ffi pins TV5/TV18 byte-exact only).

vectors.description: On an idle link (no frames for 10s) an end SHOULD send a zero-length keepalive; if no data is received for 30s the link SHOULD be considered stale and the transport MAY re-SYNC (§3.4).
USB transport properties (§4): transport id, bitmask, format, max payload, power cost, link quality, broadcast.

SYNC handshake procedure (§3.3): host opens the port and sends SYNC; the relay MUST respond with a SYNC within 1 second. The host MAY retry up to 3 times at 500 ms intervals; if there is no response after retries the port is NOT an R2 device.
Link quality on disconnect (§4.2): while the serial port is open and responsive the transport reports quality = 1.0; on disconnection it reports no link quality (peer unreachable).

Broadcast semantics (§4.3): USB is point-to-point — a frame sent on USB is delivered to exactly one peer; there is no USB broadcast. The relay node re-broadcasts on its radio transports as part of normal relay behaviour.
SYNC frame advertising v2 (type-byte mode), compact-only (§3.3). version = 0x02.
Effective version = min(host, peripheral) (§3.3). Host v2 + peripheral v1 -> link is v1 (legacy framing, §3.2): NO type byte, NO CAPS, NO control channel. A v2 endpoint MUST fall back to v1.
Host v2 + peripheral v2 -> link is v2 (type-byte framing, §3.5): the first payload byte demultiplexes R2-WIRE / CAPS / control.
An endpoint MUST NOT send type-byte-tagged frames on a link negotiated to v1 (§3.3). Sending a type-byte frame on a v1 link is non-conformant.
vectors.reason: type-byte frames forbidden on a v1-negotiated link

vectors.description: Extended is enabled ONLY by explicit BILATERAL assent (§3.3, v0.25). Both ends advertised flags bit0=1 -> extended permitted on this link establishment.

Either end advertising flags bit0=0 -> COMPACT ONLY. An endpoint MUST NOT send extended unless BOTH ends advertised bit0=1 (§3.3, v0.25). Fail-closed: compact is the default, not a fallback either side may override.

NEGOTIATION-IGNORING (§3.3, v0.25) — the third state, and the one that ships. An endpoint that sends a valid SYNC but never READS the peer's flags byte MUST behave as compact-only: it MUST NOT send extended. Ignoring a negotiation is NOT refusing one — it is declining to decide, and it is NOT a licence to skip the rule. Sending extended without having observed the peer's assent is NON-CONFORMANT.
vectors.reason: silence is not refusal; with the peer's assent unobserved the fail-closed default is compact
vectors.notes: SENDER-DRIVEN, NOT RECEIVER-CHECKABLE BY CONSTRUCTION. The falsifiable condition is peer_flags_observed=false AND attempted_send_tier=extended -> a conformant SENDER MUST refuse and emit compact; the SAME wire bytes as a negotiated-compact link cannot be used to detect ignoring from the receive side (M2, specs-codex 0abefb2). There is deliberately no matching receiver-side MUST, because none is implementable: flags bit0 is the only tier signal on a USB link and it is OUT-OF-BAND (in the §3.3 SYNC handshake, not in any frame). Compact and extended share an identical byte 0 (ver(2)|type(3)|flags(3), R2-WIRE 4.2.2 / 4.3.2; ver is the WIRE version, NOT the tier — both tiers are version 0 today), and the v2 type byte does not carry the tier (its 0x00-0xFB space is entirely local_id) -> there is NO in-frame/in-band R2-WIRE discriminator. An extended frame on a compact-negotiated link is therefore MIS-PARSED as compact, not rejected: a violating sender produces garbage that decodes, never a clean error. A conformance harness must drive this from the SENDER side.

vectors.description: v2 local_id-tagged R2-WIRE frame (§3.5, type byte 0x00-0xFB). type byte 0x00 = the transport with local_id 0; body is one R2-WIRE compact message (here the TV1 16-byte frame). usb_frame = [len LE][type][wire].
Type bytes 0xFC and 0xFD are RESERVED (§3.5) and MUST NOT be sent; a receiver MUST reject such a frame (and MAY report it via a §3.7 error control frame).

Discriminator collision-freedom (§3.5): an untagged R2-WIRE header's first byte is <= 0x3F (compact, version 0) or <= 0xBF (extended, version < 3), so it can never collide with the type-byte discriminators 0xFC-0xFF (reserved/CAPS/control). The type byte is unambiguous.

CAPS frame (§3.6, type byte 0xFE): CBOR map {0: hive_id_bytes bstr16, 1: firmware_id tstr, 2: firmware_version uint, 3: transports[ {0: local_id, 1: kind} ]}. Here: hive_id_bytes = the canonical usb_link_id (R2-USB §3.6 / cross-checks r2-usb-pair UP13 851fdee3...), firmware_id 'r2-relay', version 1, one transport {local_id 0, kind 2 = LoRa (R2-TRANSPORT §2.2 canonical enum; v0.8 unified — was 1 under the retired Appendix-A enum)}.
vectors.notes: hive_id_bytes == usb_link_id (UP13) is intentional: CAPS keys the host link-key store for pairing/reconnect (R2-PROVISION §5.3.4). Receipt of CAPS is the gate into pairing/reconnect.

vectors.description: CAPS validation (§3.6): the transports array MUST contain 1-16 descriptors, every local_id MUST be <= 0xFB, and local_id values MUST be unique. A CAPS frame violating any of these is malformed and MUST be rejected. Example: two descriptors sharing local_id 0.
vectors.reason: duplicate local_id in CAPS transports array

vectors.description: Control frame (§3.7, type byte 0xFF): CBOR {0: msg_type, 1: body}. Here msg_type 2 = log line, body tstr 'hello'.
Control frames are link-local (§3.7): they MUST NOT be relayed onto other transports. A relay node MUST consume control frames locally.

Control msg_type values 4-11 are the USB pairing flow, defined normatively in R2-PROVISION §5.3.4 (message vocabulary PAIR_HELLO_HOST(4) .. PAIR_ABORT(11)); msg_type 1 = error report, 2 = log line, 3 = transport state change; other msg_types are reserved and MUST be ignored (skipped) by receivers.

msg_types.4-11, 13, 14 (15 reserved/retired): USB pairing (R2-PROVISION §5.3.4). 13 = PAIR_HOST_REVEAL, 14 = PAIR_ACK. 15 = RESERVED (was RECONNECT_ACCEPT; retired when §5.3.4 became the SIMPLE SECURE pairing spec v0.45 — reconnect is now the two-frame same-key 9/10; 15 kept allocated, not reused).

vectors.description: v2 payload_length bound (§3.5): a host SHOULD bound the accepted payload_length (RECOMMENDED 4096, covering the R2-WIRE compact max + type-byte + CAPS overhead) and treat larger frames as malformed. NOTE: v2 type-byte frames (CAPS/control) MAY exceed the 255-byte compact limit (TV2/TV3) — that limit applies to the R2-WIRE compact MESSAGE, not to a CAPS/control payload.

Observation/sighting control frame (§3.7.1, v2 type byte 0xFF, msg_type=12): link-local peripheral->host presence telemetry, MUST-NOT-relay by frame class. Body {0:12, 1:{0:beacon, 1:bearer, 2:rssi, 3:snr}}. Here a REAL DEV LoRa beacon (17B, magic-first from 0xB2, Company-ID stripped; hive dfr1195 producer) on bearer=2 (LoRa, R2-TRANSPORT §2.2). rssi/snr are runtime-measured EXAMPLES (-67 dBm / +7 dB whole-dB) — the byte-exact conformance target is the framing + beacon bytes + enum, not the live rssi/snr.
vectors.notes: beacon 17B DEV = magic b2 · ver 01 · flags 00 · rbid 7fce111165325a9a · class bafe8ac1 (HIVE class 0xBAFE8AC1: DFR1195 is a full-service node running AP/services, R2-BEACON §4 v0.47 -- NOT a repeater) · txpwr 14 · build_class 02. PROD LoRa = 15B core (magic ver flags rbid class) OR 16B with optional tx_power; DEV = 17B (+build_class). Accepted set exactly {15,16,17} per R2-BEACON 8.1.1 — a length-gating decoder MUST accept all three. BLE (future producer) forwards the >=18B/24B manufacturer value from 0xB2 (different structure: +anti-collision+reserved), bearer=0, snr omitted.
